articles.title: Anthropic Employees Accuse Trump Administration of Targeting Them (11 minute read)
Apple Prepares Second-Generation iPhone Air for Spring 2027 (3 minute read)
articles.summary: The second-generation iPhone Air is now in advanced testing. Apple plans to launch the device in Spring 2027. The device will retain its current look, but with a second rear camera for ultrawide-angle photography and an improved battery life. It will be powered by a version of the A20 Pro processor, the same chip coming to this Fall's iPhones.

articles.title: Uneven Frontiers (52 minute read)
articles.summary: AI will make some parts of biopharma faster and cheaper, but other facets of the industry are restricted by the physical world. Recruiting patients, dosing them, and waiting for results will still take time. Drug discovery will accelerate, but clinical development will remain a bottleneck. The durable position is built around the bottleneck, not in the path of advancing discovery models.

articles.title: Mobileye Is Entering the US Robotaxi Market With Standalone Service (2 minute read)
articles.summary: Mobileye is launching a robotaxi service in the US in 2027. The vertically integrated service will use the company's Moovit mobility platform to interact with customers booking rides and coordinate drivers. It will begin service with about 100 robotaxis in a yet unnamed city. The company plans to scale up to around 17,000 robotaxis within the next five years if the initial pilot goes well.

articles.title: Lore (website)
articles.summary: Lore is a version control system optimized for projects that combine code with large binary assets. It is designed for scalability and caters to both the needs of developers and artists. Lore features a verifiable, tamper-evident source of truth, an intuitive interface, and a full-surface API. It is fully open source under an MIT license.

articles.title: Anthropic Ships Major Claude Design Overhaul With Design System Imports, Code Round-Trips, and a Fix for Its Token-Burning Problem (13 minute read)
articles.summary: Anthropic has shipped an overhauled version of Claude Design that attempts to fix its token consumption issue. With the update, Claude Design has transformed from a prototype toy to an enterprise platform. Users can now bring design systems into Claude Design from a GitHub repository, design files, or raw uploads. It can output consistent branding at speed and scale.

articles.title: Musk's Next Move May Be a Megamerger of SpaceX and Tesla (10 minute read)
articles.summary: Many of Elon Musk's fans and investors expect him to merge SpaceX with Tesla. The two companies have long shared executives and other resources and are jointly developing multibillion-dollar projects. Musk controls SpaceX and is Tesla's largest shareholder, so it could raise legal issues and prompt lawsuits if he made the deal. However, this is unlikely to stop Musk, who has donated hundreds of millions of dollars to Republican candidates, including President Donald Trump.

articles.title: Apple to Raise Prices Due to Memory Chip Crunch (7 minute read)
articles.summary: The surging costs of memory and storage chips are prompting Apple to raise its prices on its products. Apple's next major product launch is likely to be in September, but price increases could come sooner. The AI infrastructure buildout is eating up supplies for memory and storage chips. Prices have quadrupled since last year, and they're expected to continue increasing into 2027.

articles.title: Mark Zuckerberg Orders His Employees to Start Having Fun Again After Brutal Layoffs Culled Their Colleagues (3 minute read)
articles.summary: Mark Zuckerberg attempted to lift employee spirits by promising to host a companywide AI hackathon in July, but workers told him that they were in no mood for such a thing.

articles.title: You Got Faster. Your Company Didn't (3 minute read)
Why We're Bullish on Loops (5 minute read)
articles.summary: Loops allow agents to prompt themselves and improve products without input.

articles.title: Local Qwen Isn't a Worse Opus, It's a Different Tool (28 minute read)
articles.summary: Local Qwen is able to provide real value for businesses in certain tasks and workflows.

articles.title: Building a Design System Specced for Engineers and Agents (12 minute read)
articles.summary: AI makes putting together a design system less expensive than before, making the cost of building without a design foundation exponentially higher.

articles.title: AI Coding Agents Taught Robots How to Install GPUs and Cut Zip Ties (4 minute read)
articles.summary: The Nvidia GEAR lab will open-source the ENPIRE harness so that anyone can host their own self-running robot lab at home.

articles.title: Agentic coding and persistent returns to expertise (25 minute read)
Making budget models punch above their weight with a smart harness (18 minute read)
How We Made Cloud Browsers 3x Cheaper and Faster (17 minute read)
articles.summary: Browser Use's cloud browsers have been optimized to start quickly, remain isolated, and maintain low costs, now operating at $0.02 per hour with sessions starting in under a second. This was done by using Firecracker for lightweight virtual machines while implementing a control plane for dynamic scaling.

articles.title: The Dialogue Dividend (6 minute read)
articles.summary: Conversations with others often lead to greater problem-solving and understanding than solitary thinking can provide as they introduce a feedback loop that refines thoughts in real-time.

articles.title: But yak shaving is fun (8 minute read)
articles.summary: Yak shaving refers to the process of engaging in a series of tangential tasks that divert from the original goal, showing how building projects from scratch can lead to unexpected challenges, discoveries, and joy.

articles.title: Introducing eve (12 minute read)
articles.summary: eve is an open-source agent framework that makes building, running, and scaling agents easier by providing built-in production capabilities like durable execution, sandboxed computing, and human-in-the-loop approvals. With a clear structure, agents can be defined through a directory of files that dictate their components, actions, and connections.

articles.title: Lore (GitHub Repo)
articles.summary: Lore is an open source version control system designed for scalability, particularly for projects with large binary assets like games. It has a centralized, content-addressed architecture that allows for efficient data storage and retrieval, with an emphasis on immutable revisions and low-overhead branching.

articles.title: Building TREX: Code execution and artifact generation for AI code review (9 minute read)
articles.summary: TREX is a code review tool that integrates code execution with AI-driven testing to identify bugs that may not be apparent in static analysis alone. It uses a multi-agent architecture, where a Greptile reviewer orchestrates specialized TREX agents that run tests in isolated environments, providing artifacts such as screenshots and logs to verify findings.

articles.title: Image compression (35 minute read)
articles.summary: Image compression techniques, including lossy and lossless methods, exploit characteristics of human visual perception to reduce file sizes. Formats like JPEG use a combination of chroma subsampling, discrete cosine transforms, and quantization for compression without noticeable quality loss, while GIF uses indexed color palettes and Lempel-Ziv-Welch algorithms to store limited color data.

articles.title: Brain the Size of a Planet: Are LLMs Thonking too Hard? (26 minute read)
The founder's playbook: Building an AI-native startup (5 minute read)
articles.summary: Anthropic has released a playbook of practical exercises and agentic workflows to navigate every stage of the business lifecycle for AI-native startups.

articles.title: mac-ocr (GitHub Repo)
articles.summary: mac-ocr is a macOS command-line tool that uses Apple's Vision framework locally to recognize text from images and PDFs and create searchable PDFs.

articles.title: Can gzip be a language model? (5 minute read)
articles.summary: Gzip can be used as a language model through compression techniques that predict and generate text continuations by scoring potential outputs based on how well they compress with a given context.

articles.title: Exa Agent (5 minute read)
articles.summary: Exa Agent is a new API that combines frontier language models with optimized web search tools to provide high-performance research, list-building, and data enrichment capabilities.

articles.title: Amazon S3 annotations: attach rich, queryable context directly to your objects (6 minute read)
articles.summary: S3 annotations is a new Amazon S3 metadata capability that lets customers attach up to 1,000 named annotations per object (up to 1 MB each) with rich formats like JSON, XML, YAML, or plain text. Annotations are mutable (can be updated or deleted without rewriting the object) and can be surfaced at scale through S3 Metadata annotation tables queryable with Amazon Athena and Iceberg engines.

articles.title: Only half of planned 2026 US data center capacity is actually under construction (4 minute read)
articles.summary: New data suggests many announced US data center projects for 2026 and 2027 have not yet started construction. The gap highlights how AI infrastructure plans may be running ahead of real power, permitting, and construction timelines.

articles.title: World leaders want US AI, but not US control over access (3 minute read)
The Identity Crisis: Why Entity Resolution Is the Missing Foundation of Every Data Product Stack (7 minute read)
articles.summary: Many data product stacks focus on ensuring quality within individual systems but overlook a key challenge: making sure the same person or entity is recognized consistently across the organization. Teams often assume identities are already resolved, only discovering the issue when customer experiences or AI outputs conflict because different systems treat the same person as different records.

articles.title: Data primacy could build more reliable AI infrastructure (5 minute read)
articles.summary: Everpure's AI infrastructure strategy centers on a unified data plane, automation-heavy control plane, and a data intelligence layer for discovery and governance. Enterprise AI reliability depends less on models alone and more on clean, governed, accessible data.

articles.title: Okta and Google Cloud link identity to AI agents and browsers (3 minute read)
articles.summary: Okta and Google Cloud are expanding their identity platform integrations by launching Auth0 for AI Agents. They are also introducing new Chrome Enterprise integrations aimed at improving security for AI-driven workplace access and browser-based workflows.

articles.title: Vercel launches enterprise controls for agentic AI infrastructure (3 minute read)
articles.summary: Vercel introduced a new framework and enterprise controls for building agentic AI applications. The launch reflects growing demand for governance, observability, and deployment primitives around AI-native applications.

articles.title: A needle in a stack of needles: Hunting infostealers with AI (10 minute read)
articles.summary: Sophos describes a multi-layer AI/ML pipeline for identifying infostealer attacks within SOC alert streams while reducing alert fatigue. Using Taegis XDR telemetry from 11.8 trillion events over two weeks, the approach applies rule/ML detection, deduplication/correlation, suppression, and supervised ML-based alert prioritization to narrow alerts to tens of thousands and then pinpoint infostealer activity.

articles.title: SentinelOne expands Purple AI to investigate threats autonomously (3 minute read)
articles.summary: SentinelOne expanded Purple AI so it can gather evidence, connect telemetry across endpoint, identity, cloud, and third-party data, and produce an attack verdict for analysts. The tool is designed to reduce investigation time by turning security telemetry into a clearer incident narrative.

articles.title: Lighthouse for AI Agents (GitHub Repo)
articles.summary: Lighthouse for AI Agents is a Node-based tool that audits a website's AI Agent Experience readiness using 18 checks.

articles.title: Google Cloud publishes a secure MCP-on-GKE guide (4 minute read)
articles.summary: Google Cloud published a walkthrough for deploying a secure remote MCP server on Google Kubernetes Engine for agentic AI workflows.

articles.title: A 27-Year-Old Authentication Bypass in OpenBSD's PPP Stack (4 minute read)
articles.summary: Argus disclosed a PAP authentication bypass in OpenBSD's sppp(4) subsystem, where sppp_pap_input() in sys/net/if_spppsubr.c fed attacker-controlled length fields straight into bcmp, so a PPPoE peer sending name_len=0 and passwd_len=0 made both comparisons return 0 and complete authentication with no credentials, while oversized lengths triggered a kernel heap over-read. Reachable unauthenticated over the PPPoE data path, a rogue server in the same broadcast domain can impersonate a legitimate endpoint and route a victim's traffic through it. The flaw dates to the 1999 FreeBSD-derived import and was verified against OpenBSD 7.6. The fix adds the exact-length pre-check the CHAP handler already used, so administrators running PPPoE links on sppp(4) should pull the patched source and rebuild.

articles.title: Sweeping Credential-Harvesting Heist Compromises 30K+ Fortinet Devices (4 minute read)
articles.summary: An automated campaign called FortiBleed is using reused and long-lived credentials to break into over 30,000 Internet-facing Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways across 194 countries, with more than 30,791 verified device logins tied to 21,108 IPs and 8,316 domains in telecom, government, critical infrastructure, and other sectors. Once a device is accessed, attackers turn it into a listening post to grab more passwords, feeding them back into their scanner. Technical evidence points to Russian-speaking actors prioritizing NATO-country targets.

articles.title: FortiBleed Leak Exposes Fortinet VPN Credentials for 73K Devices (2 minute read)
articles.summary: A security researcher discovered a server that contained credentials from an ongoing Fortinet bruteforce campaign. The server contained nearly 74K usernames, passwords, and email addresses for Fortinet VPN appliances that the attacker harvested through brute force attacks. The researcher also stated that the attacker also obtained credentials by brute forcing Microsoft SQL Server databases and cracking intercepted SSL VPN hashes.

articles.title: On Post-Quantum Security Adoption (5 minute read)
articles.summary: Citing 2029 quantum-readiness targets from Google and Cloudflare and a 2035 target from the UK's NCSC, this post walks through hardening three everyday protocols against harvest-now-decrypt-later attacks using hybrid classical/post-quantum key exchange. OpenSSH has defaulted to mlkem768x25519-sha256 since April 2022, while TLS uses X25519MLKEM768 (supported in Firefox since 132 and Chrome since 131), now covering an estimated 70.1% of Internet traffic per Cloudflare Radar. WireGuard, not post-quantum secure by default, can mix a symmetric PresharedKey for basic protection or layer Rosenpass on top to rotate the preshared key every two minutes.

articles.title: QoS Policies (7 minute read)
articles.summary: Windows QoS policies can cap outbound bandwidth for specific processes, ports, or protocols, and attackers with admin rights can point these policies at EDR agents to choke their telemetry to the vendor cloud, sometimes down to 8 bits per second so TLS handshakes fail and alerts never arrive. The idea is to create such policies via PowerShell and WMI, with the EDRChoker proof-of-concept that mass-creates throttling rules for common EDR processes. Defensive playbooks include auditing ActiveStore QoS entries with PowerShell cmdlets, enabling detailed PowerShell and WMI logging, and monitoring both transient (memory) and persistent (registry) QoS policies.

articles.title: Inside Claude Managed Agents: Reverse Engineering the Security Boundaries of Anthropic's Hosted Agent Runtime (12 minute read)
articles.summary: Pluto Security reverse-engineered Anthropic's Claude Managed Agents hosted-agent runtime to probe its security features and limitations. Pluto found that the runtime uses the same gVisor sandbox as Cloud Cowork, the vault credential proxy provides strong secrets isolation, and network proxy bypasses utilize a multi-layer defense to prevent them. Pluto also noted that while there are strong security configurations, the default configuration is very permissive, and teams should ensure agents are secured thoroughly.

articles.title: Chainguard, JPMorgan, BNY Team Up to Secure Open Source from AI Threats (2 minute read)
articles.summary: Chainguard launched Athena, an industry coalition (founding members include BNY, Cisco, Cloudflare, Docker, JPMorganChase, Kyndryl, LTIMindtree, and PwC) that pools open-source vulnerabilities surfaced by frontier AI programs like Anthropic's Project Glasswing and OpenAI's Daybreak, patches them privately, and rebuilds affected projects as hardened versions ahead of coordinated upstream disclosure. Already operational, Athena has processed over 20,000 findings and shipped more than 2,000 patches across 500 open-source projects, with its first disclosure wave due in July.

articles.title: Magnitude (Product Launch)
articles.summary: Magnitude builds an autonomous workforce of AI agents that manage third-party and Nth-party risk for companies by continuously assessing vendor security, mapping dependencies, and triggering remediation when vulnerabilities or supply chain threats appear.

articles.title: Cybersecurity Certification Demand (GitHub Repo)
articles.summary: This repository contains a web scraper that pulls cybersecurity job postings from public hiring APIs, filters for genuine cyber roles, and reports which certifications employers most frequently list.

articles.title: China-Nexus Actor Spies on US Researchers Undetected for a Year (5 minute read)
articles.summary: Google's GTIG and Mandiant attributed a year-plus espionage campaign against US medical, academic, and military research institutions to a new China-aligned actor, UNC6508, which used REDCap-specific malware named Infinitered to harvest credentials, then exfiltrated email by abusing domain content-compliance rules rather than malware. What stands out is an unusually broad collection scope at a single site, malware reverse-engineered to run only on REDCap servers, and an obfuscation network built exclusively from US-based IPs to blend in with normal logins.

articles.title: How We Cut Semgrep's Taint Analysis Time by 75% (6 minute read)
articles.summary: To enable interfile taint tracking, Semgrep Pro historically ran taint analysis twice. With the release of improved multicore support in OCaml 5.0, the Semgrep team was able to rearchitect interfile taint tracking to run only once. Using a custom OCaml profiler, the team recorded speedups of up to 75% with this refactor.

articles.title: Peter Thiel's Dialog Network Was Super Secret. A Data Leak Changed That (4 minute read)
articles.summary: Dialog is a private, invite-only network of elites from business, academia, and politics that Peter Thiel founded. A security researcher discovered a set of leaked data from Dialog that included 113 names listed on Dialog's private website, as well as participant profiles containing names, employers, locations, email addresses, assistants' contact information, birthdates, phone numbers, emergency contacts, and other information for attendees.

articles.title: Estonia to Quarantine Emails Sent from Russian .RU Domain Before They Reach Government Officials (2 minute read)
articles.summary: Starting August 31, Estonia will automatically quarantine inbound emails from Russia's .ru top-level domain before they reach public-sector officials, notifying recipients to open isolated messages only with added precautions, a measure justified by the sharp rise since 2022 in phishing and malware traffic from Russian servers.

articles.title: JetBrains Marketplace Ecosystem Security Update: Addressing Malicious Third-Party AI Plugins (4 minute read)
articles.summary: JetBrains disclosed and purged 15 malicious third-party Marketplace plugins from 7 banned publisher accounts.

articles.title: Cyberattack Sees Crops Kept in the Ground (2 minute read)
articles.summary: Mackay Sugar in Queensland had to halt most sugar cane crushing after a cyberattack disrupted systems that manage harvesting, mill operations, and logistics.

articles.title: ChatGPT's Market Share Slips Below 50% For First Time (4 minute read)
articles.summary: ChatGPT's market share has dipped below 50% for the first time. Users are migrating to other assistants like Google's Gemini, Anthropic's Claude, and xAI's Grok. ChatGPT was the fastest app to ever reach a billion monthly users, and it still remains the most popular AI assistant worldwide. Users are increasingly willing to switch between assistants.

articles.title: ChatGPT Improves Scheduled Tasks And Retires Pulse (1 minute read)
articles.summary: ChatGPT has introduced an improved task scheduling feature, enhancing speed and reliability. It is accessible via the new Scheduled page and available to Go, Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise users.

articles.title: Brain The Size Of A Planet: Are LLMs Thonking Too Hard? (30 minute read)
articles.summary: Earlier studies showed that Opus 4.6, GPT 5.4, Gemini 3.1-pro-preview, Deepseek R1-0528, and Qwen 3.6-plus were unable to find two vulnerabilities without extremely revealing hints. This study continues the experiments with 26 distinct model combinations and different context window sizes and reasoning efforts. It found that higher reasoning effort, and even later models, are not always better for triaging security results.

articles.title: Kimi K2.7 Code vs Claude Fable 5: Landing Pages That Cost 94% Less (6 minute read)
articles.summary: An experiment compared Kimi K2.7 Code with Claude Fable 5 to generate 12 landing pages each. Kimi K2.7 Code cut costs by 94%, being 16 times cheaper than Claude Fable 5.

articles.title: Vercel Connect (8 minute read)
articles.summary: Vercel launched Connect in public beta, replacing long-lived provider tokens with runtime credential exchange that issues short-lived, task-scoped credentials for agents. The approach reduces the risk associated with shared, persistent access tokens.

articles.title: Production Infrastructure For AI Agents (19 minute read)
articles.summary: Vercel introduced eve, an open-source agent framework with built-in capabilities such as durable execution, sandboxed compute, approvals, subagents, and evaluations. It aims to let developers focus on agent behavior while the framework handles production concerns.

articles.title: Building AI Agents For AR Glasses And XR Devices With NVIDIA XR AI (12 minute read)
articles.summary: Nvidia XR AI provides a reusable foundation for connecting extended reality devices to GPU-accelerated AI services running in the cloud, data center, workstation, or edge. It gives developers access to an open source library for building intelligent agents for AI glasses, AR glasses, and XR headsets. These agents will be able to see what users see, understand token or typed intent, call enterprise tools, and respond within the same XR session. XR AI is now available in public beta.

articles.title: MolmoMotion: Language-Guided 3D Motion Forecasting (9 minute read)
articles.summary: MolmoMotion, a new motion forecasting model, predicts future 3D point trajectories in videos using language instructions and initial object positions, outperforming existing methods. The model and its dataset, MolmoMotion-1M, feature vast datasets of 3D point trajectories from 1.16M videos, with a benchmark for accuracy testing.

articles.title: Cursor's New Model (1 minute read)
articles.summary: Cursor's new model will push agentic software development far beyond autocomplete and pair programming. The massive model was trained from scratch on over 100,000 GPUs. It has more than 1.5 trillion parameters and delivers intelligence beyond coding tasks. The model will be released in the coming weeks.

articles.title: Noam Shazeer Is Joining OpenAI (1 minute read)
articles.summary: Noam Shazeer is leaving Google to join OpenAI. Shazeer was one of the co-authors of the 2017 paper "Attention Is All You Need" that transformed the AI world. He also improved search spelling correction and created the core algorithm for AdSense.

articles.title: Mistral Has A New Model Coming This Summer (2 minute read)
articles.summary: An early access program will be opened in July for key partners in research, government, and the industry.

articles.title: Introducing LifeSciBench (7 minute read)
articles.summary: OpenAI introduced LifeSciBench, an expert-judged benchmark that evaluates AI systems on end-to-end life sciences workflows such as evidence analysis, experimental design, scientific reasoning, and research communication rather than isolated biology tasks.

articles.title: Replit Is Now Available In Claude (2 minute read)
articles.summary: Replit is now integrated into Claude, allowing seamless transitions from design to development.
